title: rubus ratiboriensis
intro: Rubus ratiboriensis, also known as the Tibetan raspberry or Sikkim raspberry, is a species of raspberry native to the Himalayan region. This plant is rich in antioxidants and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Rubus ratiboriensis include its potential to reduce blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and support digestive health. It is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made from the leaves, stems, and roots of the plant.
